title: Contributing
text: function variable names, exported internal, use snake_case. always true: exported functions used mix camelCase snake_case (e.g. eselistFromYAML() vs. eselistfromConfig()), several plotting functions named library renders (plotly_scatterplot(), ggplot_boxplot()) rather . plotly_* prefix particular collided spirit plotly‘s exports (plotly_build(), plotly_json(), etc.), autocomplete skim exported symbols couldn’t tell shinyngs’ wrappers plotly’s API. whole surface renamed one pass (major version bump shipped ) rather left calcify — don’t re-introduce camelCase implementation-named functions new code. Plotting functions named produce, library used render : interactive_* plotly-backed versions, static_* ggplot2/scatterplot3d-backed ones (e.g. interactive_boxplot() / static_boxplot()). exceptions snake_case intentional, oversights: S4 class constructors (ExploratorySummarizedExperiment, ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList) stay PascalCase, matching standing Bioconductor/S4 convention shared SummarizedExperiment friends. Shiny module passes reactive argument, prefix parameter name get (getTitle, getPalette, getColorby) distinguish plain value concept (e.g. colorby column-name string parameter used plotting/builder functions modules call). Don’t let name mean “reactive” one layer “plain value” another. getExtension()/getSeparator() used violate (plain functions returning plain value, reactive) — ’re now file_extension()/guess_separator().

title: Contributing
text: app built Shiny modules. module xxx trio functions, normally R/xxx.R: xxxInput(id, eselist) builds sidebar controls, xxxOutput(id) builds display slot (plot table), xxx(id, eselist) server, called directly wrapping logic shiny::moduleServer(). Modules compose rather read eselist directly. Data selection (experiment, assay, rows, columns) comes selectmatrix module, whose server returns reactives selectMatrix(), selectColData() getExperiment(); colour-grouping palette picker come groupby module (getGroupby(), getPalette()). new plot module gets free embedding submodules. plotting module thin adapter: actual drawing lives exported, Shiny-free standalone function (example boxplot module calls static_boxplot() / interactive_boxplot()). Put new drawing logic exported interactive_* / static_* function takes plain data give palette_name = COLORBLIND_PALETTE_NAME default, module call . full walkthrough (including step--step “add new module” recipe) see developer guide article, vignettes/articles/developer.Rmd.

what: Preserve bindCache() scope when extracting render logic
title: Contributing
text: Several module servers wrap plot-building reactive bindCache(...), keyed exactly inputs reactive reads, re-rendering tab (another session viewing matrix settings) reuses result instead recomputing. extract render logic module server standalone function (preferred direction), make sure bindCache() key still covers every input drawing now depends . Miss one either serve stale output silently defeat cache.

what: Running tests, lints and the document workflow
title: Contributing
text: pushing, run checks CI : Regenerate documentation NAMESPACE roxygen comments whenever add change exported function, commit results: Run devtools::document() committing NAMESPACE man/ stay sync source. New exported functions need complete roxygen block (@param, @return, @examples, @export); module-trio functions follow existing convention @keywords shiny generally left unexported.
code: # Tests devtools::test()  # Lints and style lintr::lint_package() styler::style_pkg()   # or check formatting without rewriting devtools::document()

title: Building an app from files and YAML
text: eselist_from_yaml(".yaml") reads manifest describes study points data files. builds exactly object build hand, slots straight prepare_app(): representative manifest: Mapping back data model: entry experiments becomes one ExploratorySummarizedExperiment (can describe transcript- gene-level experiments side side); coldata, annotation expression_matrices populate ESE’s colData, annotation assays; id/label set idfield/labelfield; contrasts stats populate study contrasts per-ESE contrast_stats. Useful optional top-level keys: ensembl_species (e.g. hsapiens, mmusculus) - set, annotation supplies chromosome_name/start_position/end_position, enables igv.js gene-model view gene module. static_pdf - switch static plot downloads PDF. url_roots - add link-outs identifier columns. gene_set_id_type gene_sets (named list .gmt paths, one per collection) - YAML-driven alternative supplying gene sets via R constructor. zhangneurons example dataset generated.
code: library(shinyngs) eselist <- eselist_from_yaml("my.yaml") app <- prepare_app("rnaseq", eselist) shiny::shinyApp(app$ui, app$server) title: My RNA-seq experiment author: Joe Bloggs report: report.md ensembl_species: hsapiens group_vars:   - Group   - Replicate default_groupvar: Group experiments:   Gene:     coldata:       file: samplesheet.csv       id: sample     annotation:       file: gene_meta.csv       id: gene_id       label: gene_name     expression_matrices:       Raw:         file: raw_counts.csv         measure: counts       Normalised:         file: normalised_counts.csv         measure: Counts per million     read_reports:       read_attrition: read_attrition.csv contrasts:   comparisons:     0:       - Group       - control       - treated stats:   Gene:     Normalised:       pvals: pvals.csv       qvals: qvals.csv

title: Building an app from files and YAML
text: exec/make_app_from_files.R builds app bundle flat files without writing R. path used pipelines nf-core/differentialabundance, calls produce Shiny report end run. minimal invocation: writes app.R serialised data.rds app/. flags map onto data model: --assay_files / --assay_names - expression matrices display names. --sample_metadata / --sample_id_col - sample sheet identifier column. --feature_metadata / --feature_id_col / --feature_name_col - feature annotation columns idfield labelfield. --contrast_file - CSV whose first three columns contrast variable, reference target, one row per contrast. --differential_results - one differential table per contrast row, read fold changes p-values (columns named --fold_change_column, --pval_column, --qval_column). --contrast_stats_assay - 1-based index assay associate statistics (usually normalised matrix). --fold_change_scale - log2, linear auto; auto infers scale column name value distribution errors signals disagree. complete flag reference, including grouping variables, gene-model support (--ensembl_species) shinyapps.io deployment (--deploy_app), see CLI reference.
code: make_app_from_files.R \   --assay_files raw.tsv,normalised_counts.tsv \   --assay_names Raw,Normalised \   --sample_metadata samplesheet.csv \   --feature_metadata gene_meta.tsv \   --contrast_file contrasts.csv \   --differential_results treatment-saline-drug.deseq2.results.tsv \   --contrast_stats_assay 2 \   --fold_change_scale log2 \   --output_directory app

title: Building an app from files and YAML
text: output directory standard Shiny app. Run locally: run via Docker without installing anything. Biocontainers image ships shinyngs Shiny ready serve generated app.R/data.rds. latest tag, pick recent tag list mount output directory: browse http://localhost:3838.
code: library(shinyngs) shiny::runApp("app") docker run --rm -p 3838:3838 \   -v "$(pwd)/app":/data -w /data \   quay.io/biocontainers/r-shinyngs:<latest-tag> \   Rscript -e "shiny::runApp('/data', host = '0.0.0.0', port = 3838)"

title: Building an app from files and YAML
text: make_app_from_files.R can also attach GSEA (roast) results command line. --enrichment_filename_template describes result files live, {contrast_name} {geneset_type} substituted per contrast gene-set type, plus optional {target|reference} token points split /GSEA files: Notes: --enrichment_gene_sets takes comma-separated list GMT files used analysis; --enrichment_gene_type_id names identifier type use (e.g. gene_name Entrez ID). --enrichment_skip_missing tolerates contrasts gene-set types result file; simply show “enrichment results contrast” app. tool auto-detected, name columns filter --enrichment_pval_column, --enrichment_fdr_column --enrichment_direction_column. Auto-detection roast/GSEA formats described data model article.
code: make_app_from_files.R \   ... \   --contrast_file contrasts.csv \   --enrichment_gene_sets my_gene_sets.gmt \   --enrichment_filename_template 'gsea_{contrast_name}_{geneset_type}_for_{target|reference}.tsv'

title: Building an app from files and YAML
text: exec/ directory holds scripts reuse shinyngs’ plotting validation code outside Shiny app. takes --help: exploratory_plots.R - static exploratory plots (PCA, boxplots, densities) assay files sample metadata. differential_plots.R - static differential plots (volcano) differential-results files. validate_fom_components.R - runs shinyngs’ consistency checks (matrices vs. sample/feature annotation vs. contrasts) file complement without building app, feature/observation-matrix pipeline can fail fast inconsistent inputs. See CLI reference full argument set .

title: Command-line interface reference
text: Assembles full ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList flat files writes ready--run Shiny app bundle (data.rds plus app.R) output directory, optionally deploying shinyapps.io. entry point nf-core/differentialabundance pipeline uses. matrices metadata produces exploratory-app; adding contrast file plus differential results wires differential views, adding enrichment options wires gene-set enrichment. Mandatory arguments: --title, --author, --sample_metadata, --sample_id_col, --feature_metadata, --feature_id_col, --diff_feature_id_col, --assay_files, --assay_entity_name, --output_directory, --contrast_stats_assay, --differential_results. (mandatory list includes differential/contrast arguments, provide contrast file differential results even largely exploratory app.)

title: Command-line interface reference
text: three --enrichment_*_column flags must supplied together .

title: Command-line interface reference
text: --deploy_app set, --shinyapps_account --shinyapps_name required, along SHINYAPPS_TOKEN SHINYAPPS_SECRET environment variables.

title: Command-line interface reference
code: Rscript exec/make_app_from_files.R \   --title "My RNA-seq study" \   --author "Analysis team" \   --sample_metadata samplesheet.csv \   --sample_id_col sample \   --feature_metadata gene_metadata.tsv \   --feature_id_col gene_id \   --feature_name_col gene_name \   --assay_files raw_counts.tsv,normalised_counts.tsv,vst_counts.tsv \   --assay_names "Raw counts,Normalised,Variance-stabilised" \   --contrast_stats_assay 2 \   --diff_feature_id_col gene_id \   --contrast_file contrasts.csv \   --differential_results treatment_vs_control.deseq2.results.tsv \   --output_directory my_app

title: Command-line interface reference
code: Rscript exec/make_app_from_files.R \   --title "My RNA-seq study" \   --author "Analysis team" \   --sample_metadata samplesheet.csv \   --sample_id_col sample \   --feature_metadata gene_metadata.tsv \   --feature_id_col gene_id \   --feature_name_col gene_name \   --assay_files raw_counts.tsv,normalised_counts.tsv,vst_counts.tsv \   --assay_names "Raw counts,Normalised,Variance-stabilised" \   --contrast_stats_assay 2 \   --diff_feature_id_col gene_id \   --contrast_file contrasts.csv \   --differential_results treatment_vs_control.deseq2.results.tsv,mutant_vs_wildtype.deseq2.results.tsv \   --fold_change_column log2FoldChange \   --pval_column pvalue \   --qval_column padj \   --enrichment_gene_sets h.all.v2023.symbols.gmt,c2.cp.reactome.v2023.symbols.gmt \   --enrichment_filename_template "{contrast_name}.{geneset_type}.gsea_report.tsv" \   --enrichment_gene_type_id gene_name \   --ensembl_species hsapiens \   --output_directory my_app

what: Running and deploying the generated app
title: Command-line interface reference
text: output directory self-contained Shiny app. Run locally : deploy shinyapps.io part build, add deployment flags set credentials environment:
code: Rscript -e 'shiny::runApp("my_app")' export SHINYAPPS_TOKEN=... SHINYAPPS_SECRET=... Rscript exec/make_app_from_files.R \   ... \   --output_directory my_app \   --deploy_app \   --shinyapps_account myaccount \   --shinyapps_name my-rnaseq-study

title: Command-line interface reference
text: Renders standalone exploratory plots (boxplots, density plots, 2D/3D PCA, sample clustering dendrogram, MAD-score outlier plots) expression matrices sample metadata. PNGs always written <outdir>/png; --write_html, interactive plotly HTML versions also written <outdir>/html. Mandatory arguments: --assay_files, --sample_metadata, --feature_metadata, --contrast_variable, --outdir.

title: Command-line interface reference
code: Rscript exec/exploratory_plots.R \   --assay_files raw_counts.tsv,vst_counts.tsv \   --assay_names "Raw counts,Variance-stabilised" \   --final_assay "Variance-stabilised" \   --sample_metadata samplesheet.csv \   --sample_id_col sample \   --feature_metadata gene_metadata.tsv \   --feature_id_col gene_id \   --feature_name_col gene_name \   --contrast_variable treatment \   --n_genes 500 \   --write_html \   --outdir exploratory

title: Command-line interface reference
text: Renders standalone differential plots (volcano plot) single differential results table feature metadata. PNG written <outdir>/png/volcano.png; --write_html, interactive plotly version written <outdir>/html/volcano.html. Mandatory arguments: --differential_file, --feature_metadata, --outdir, --reference_level, --treatment_level.

title: Command-line interface reference

text: Building app files - prose walk-file-based workflow make_app_from_files.R automates. nf-core/differentialabundance integration (#127) calls make_app_from_files.R build interactive app distributes reports.

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: shinyngs defines two S4 classes: ExploratorySummarizedExperiment (ESE) extends Bioconductor’s SummarizedExperiment. Like parent holds one assays (feature x sample matrices) plus sample metadata (colData) feature annotation (rowData/mcols). adds slots describing label features find per-contrast statistics, gene-set analyses read reports. ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList (ESEList) list one ESEs sharing samples potentially different feature sets (example transcript-level gene-level quantification experiment). carries study-wide metadata: title, author, grouping variables, contrasts used differential testing, gene sets link-URLs. division labour matters. Anything specific one feature set (matrices, annotation, differential statistics) lives ESE. Anything describes study whole (contrasts, gene sets, grouping variables) lives ESEList. contrast definition ESEList referenced contrast_stats every contained ESE.

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: build tiny toy dataset: 6 genes across 4 samples (two groups two replicates). Everything evaluated page built, can copy code get working object.

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: assay matrix features rows samples columns. can supply several assays representing different stages processing features samples - raw count matrix normalised (CPM) matrix. column names every assay must match row names colData. names give list elements (raw, normalised) app shows assay selector, choose humans.
code: set.seed(1) genes <- paste0("GENE", 1:6) samples <- c("ctrl_1", "ctrl_2", "trt_1", "trt_2")  raw_counts <- matrix(   sample(0:500, 24, replace = TRUE),   nrow = 6, ncol = 4,   dimnames = list(genes, samples) )  normalised <- sweep(raw_counts, 2, colSums(raw_counts), "/") * 1e6  myassays <- list(raw = raw_counts, normalised = round(normalised, 2)) myassays$raw #>       ctrl_1 ctrl_2 trt_1 trt_2 #> GENE1    323    269   276   328 #> GENE2    166    465   361    78 #> GENE3    128    186   437   212 #> GENE4    417    306   493    36 #> GENE5    470    480   329   104 #> GENE6    298     84   262   216

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: colData sample sheet: one row per sample, columns describing experimental design. row names identify samples must match assay column names.
code: mycoldata <- data.frame(   Group = c("control", "control", "treated", "treated"),   Replicate = c("1", "2", "1", "2"),   row.names = samples ) mycoldata #>          Group Replicate #> ctrl_1 control         1 #> ctrl_2 control         2 #> trt_1  treated         1 #> trt_2  treated         2

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: Feature annotation data frame one row per feature. important shinyngs: supplies human-readable labels used throughout app , optionally, genomic coordinates unlock gene-model view. row names must match assay row names. Three annotation columns singled name build object: idfield - column whose values equal assay row names (primary identifier, e.g. Ensembl gene ID). labelfield - column used display labels (e.g. gene symbol). also key shinyngs uses relate gene sets features. entrezgenefield - column holding Entrez IDs, used link-outs gene-set operations. Optional. Including chromosome_name, start_position end_position (plus setting ensembl_species ESEList, ) enables interactive igv.js gene model view gene module.
code: myannotation <- data.frame(   gene_id = genes,   gene_name = paste0("Symbol", 1:6),   entrezgene = as.character(1000 + 1:6),   chromosome_name = "1",   start_position = seq(1, by = 1000, length.out = 6),   end_position = seq(500, by = 1000, length.out = 6),   row.names = genes,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) myannotation #>       gene_id gene_name entrezgene chromosome_name start_position end_position #> GENE1   GENE1   Symbol1       1001               1              1          500 #> GENE2   GENE2   Symbol2       1002               1           1001         1500 #> GENE3   GENE3   Symbol3       1003               1           2001         2500 #> GENE4   GENE4   Symbol4       1004               1           3001         3500 #> GENE5   GENE5   Symbol5       1005               1           4001         4500 #> GENE6   GENE6   Symbol6       1006               1           5001         5500

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: ExploratorySummarizedExperiment() ties together. Note assays wrapped SimpleList() colData DataFrame(), exactly plain SummarizedExperiment. constructor aligns every assay annotation common set row names, coerces annotation character rounds assay values two decimal places, minor inconsistencies inputs reconciled .
code: myese <- ExploratorySummarizedExperiment(   assays = SimpleList(myassays),   colData = DataFrame(mycoldata),   annotation = myannotation,   idfield = "gene_id",   labelfield = "gene_name",   entrezgenefield = "entrezgene" ) myese #> class: ExploratorySummarizedExperiment  #> dim: 6 4  #> metadata(0): #> assays(2): raw normalised #> rownames(6): GENE1 GENE2 ... GENE5 GENE6 #> rowData names(6): gene_id gene_name ... start_position end_position #> colnames(4): ctrl_1 ctrl_2 trt_1 trt_2 #> colData names(2): Group Replicate

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: single ESE directly viewable; must live inside ESEList, supplies study-level context. point already runnable app. chunk launches Shiny marked eval=FALSE browser session run page build, code exactly run interactively: group_vars names colData columns user may colour group samples (PCA plot, boxplots ). omit group_vars, constructor guesses sensible ones colData; supplying explicitly gives control appears.
code: myesel <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(   eses = list(expression = myese),   title = "Toy study",   author = "A. Bioinformatician",   description = "A tiny worked example",   group_vars = c("Group", "Replicate"),   default_groupvar = "Group" ) #> Creating ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList object myesel@group_vars #> [1] "Group"     "Replicate" app <- prepare_app("rnaseq", myesel) shiny::shinyApp(app$ui, app$server)

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: Differential-expression panels (tables, volcano plots, MA plots, fold-change scatters) appear object describes contrasts. contrast length-3 character vector: colData variable, value control side, value treatment side. Contrasts live ESEList property study. contrasts defined statistics, app can show group means fold changes computed fly. draw volcano plots filter significance also populate contrast_stats ESE. list keyed assay name; element list matrices named pvals, qvals (optionally) fold_changes. Every matrix features rows one column per contrast, order ESEList’s contrasts slot. Keying contrast_stats normalised means app relates statistics normalised assay drawing plots - usually want, since fold changes p-values computed normalised data. fold_changes omitted derived fly group means.
code: myesel@contrasts <- list(   c("Group", "control", "treated") ) pvals <- matrix(runif(6), ncol = 1, dimnames = list(genes, NULL)) qvals <- matrix(runif(6), ncol = 1, dimnames = list(genes, NULL)) fold_changes <- matrix(rnorm(6), ncol = 1, dimnames = list(genes, NULL))  myese@contrast_stats <- list(   normalised = list(pvals = pvals, qvals = qvals, fold_changes = fold_changes) )  # Put the updated ESE back into the list myesel[[1]] <- myese names(myesel[[1]]@contrast_stats$normalised) #> [1] "pvals"        "qvals"        "fold_changes"

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: Many views useful restricted biologically meaningful set genes. ESEList gene_sets slot holds . Internally list keyed first labelfield (identifier type used display), collection name, set name, set character vector feature labels: rarely build structure hand. practice read .gmt files (example MSigDB) GeneSetCollections pass constructor via gene_sets argument together gene_set_id_type (annotation column GMT identifiers correspond ). constructor translates GMT identifiers labelfield labels builds nested structure : gene_set_id_type = "entrezgene" tells constructor GMT files use Entrez IDs, maps onto entrezgene annotation column relabels gene_name display.
code: myesel@gene_sets <- list(   gene_name = list(     my_collection = list(       set_A = c("Symbol1", "Symbol2", "Symbol3"),       set_B = c("Symbol4", "Symbol5")     )   ) ) names(myesel@gene_sets$gene_name) #> [1] "my_collection" genesets_files <- list(   "KEGG" = "/path/to/c2.cp.kegg.v5.0.entrez.gmt",   "MSigDB hallmark" = "/path/to/h.all.v5.0.entrez.gmt" ) gene_sets <- lapply(genesets_files, GSEABase::getGmt)  myesel <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(   eses = list(expression = myese),   title = "Toy study",   gene_sets = gene_sets,   gene_set_id_type = "entrezgene" )

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: Pre-computed enrichment results stored ESE gene_set_analyses slot, supplied via constructor argument name. three-level nested list keyed assay, gene-set type, contrast; leaf data frame enrichment results (NULL contrast none):
code: gene_set_analyses <- list(   normalised = list(     my_collection = list(       "control-treated" = read.delim("roast_results.tsv")     )   ) )

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: Different enrichment tools name p-value, FDR direction columns differently. shinyngs inspects table detects tool, roast/mroast output (PValue, FDR, Direction) GSEA output (NOM p-val, FDR q-val) filtered correct columns without extra configuration. Every table validated resolved tool’s expected columns ESE constructed, malformed enrichment file fails fast actionable error rather breaking user opens contrast. optional gene_set_analyses_tool slot (nested identically gene_set_analyses) forces particular format; defaults "auto". support tool shinyngs recognise natively, set corresponding entry named vector giving columns filter instead tool name: GSEA reports - -regulated results two files. assemble object files, supply named /pair per contrast shinyngs combines , adding Direction column.
code: gene_set_analyses_tool <- list(   normalised = list(     my_collection = list(       "control-treated" = c(pvalue = "PValue", fdr = "adj.P.Val", direction = "dir")     )   ) )

title: The data model: building ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects
text: assemble object R. file- YAML-driven route reads standard file complement (matrices, sample sheet, contrasts, differential results) builds ESEList . Individual displays can run isolation, modules combined bespoke apps - see reusing components. full inventory panel needs object, see module catalog.

title: Developer guide: how modules work and how to add one
text: shinyngs visualises data held ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects (SummarizedExperiment extra descriptive slots), grouped ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList (eselist) represents whole study. Almost every feature app Shiny module: self-contained, namespaced unit UI plus server logic can reused anywhere without inputs outputs colliding another instance. Top-level apps rnaseq assembled many modules.

title: Developer guide: how modules work and how to add one
text: core convention: module xxx defined three functions, usually living R/xxx.R. xxxInput(id, eselist) returns UI controls (sidebar form elements). xxxOutput(id) returns UI displays results (typically plot table output slot). xxx(id, eselist) server function. called directly id wraps logic shiny::moduleServer(). dendro module compact example. dendroInput() builds clustering controls, dendroOutput() returns plot slot, dendro() server: Note shape: Namespacing explicit. UI functions call ns <- NS(id) wrap every id ns(...); server calls moduleServer(id, ...) Shiny handles namespace . Input Output functions must agree server inner ids ("dendro", "sampleDendroPlot"), three halves find . Modules compose. dendroInput() embeds selectmatrixInput() groupbyInput(); dendro() embeds matching selectmatrix() groupby() servers.
code: dendroInput <- function(id, eselist) {   ns <- NS(id)   expression_filters <- selectmatrixInput(ns("dendro"), eselist)   dendro_filters <- list(     selectInput(ns("corMethod"), "Correlation method", c(Pearson = "pearson", ...)),     selectInput(ns("clusterMethod"), "Clustering method", c(...)),     groupbyInput(ns("dendro"))   )   fieldSets(ns("fieldset"), list(clustering = dendro_filters, expression = expression_filters)) }  dendroOutput <- function(id) {   ns <- NS(id)   moduleMain(     "Sample clustering dendrogram",     shinycssloaders::withSpinner(plotlyOutput(ns("sampleDendroPlot"), height = "480px"), color = shinyngsSpinnerColor()),     help = modalInput(ns(dendro_modal$id), "help", "help")   ) }  dendro <- function(id, eselist) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     modalServer(dendro_modal$id, dendro_modal$title)     selectmatrix_reactives <- selectmatrix("dendro", eselist, select_genes = TRUE, var_n = 1000, ...)     groupby_reactives <- groupby("dendro", eselist = eselist, group_label = "Color by", selectColData = selectmatrix_reactives$selectColData)     # ... build the plot from those reactives, render to output$sampleDendroPlot   }) }

title: Developer guide: how modules work and how to add one
text: module passes reactive another function, parameter name prefixed get (getTitle, getPalette, getColorby, getNumberCategories). distinguishes reactive plain value concept (example colorby column-name string plotting function takes). Keep distinct: don’t let name mean “reactive” one layer “plain value” another.

what: How a module gets its data: selectmatrix
title: Developer guide: how modules work and how to add one
text: modules don’t read eselist directly. Instead delegate data selection selectmatrix module, compositional core package. selectmatrixInput() supplies controls choose: ExploratorySummarizedExperiment list use, assay experiment, rows (via geneselect submodule) columns (via sampleselect). selectmatrix() server parses inputs returns named list reactives downstream modules consume. -used ones: groupby module ’ll almost always compose : provides “colour ” control (embedding colormaker palette picker) returns getGroupby() (chosen column name) getPalette() (reactive palette). shared modules, new plot module automatically inherits experiment/assay/row/column selection, colour-grouping, colour-blind-safe palette, just composing .
code: selectmatrix_reactives <- selectmatrix("myMatrix", eselist, select_genes = TRUE)  selectmatrix_reactives$selectMatrix()      # the selected (row/col-filtered) matrix selectmatrix_reactives$selectColData()     # colData for the selected samples selectmatrix_reactives$getExperiment()     # the chosen ExploratorySummarizedExperiment selectmatrix_reactives$getAssayMeasure()   # assay label, e.g. for axis titles selectmatrix_reactives$matrixTitle()       # a human-readable title for the selection

title: Developer guide: how modules work and how to add one
text: shinyngs plotting module intentionally thin. actual plot drawn exported, standalone function takes plain data (matrix, data frame, colorby column name, palette) knows nothing Shiny. module’s job gather reactives call function. boxplot module (R/boxplot.R): static_boxplot() draws static ggplot2 boxplot. interactive_boxplot() draws interactive plotly version. interactive_quartiles() / interactive_densityplot() alternative renderings. server just wires reactives : split deliberate pattern, recent direction codebase extract render logic module servers exported standalone functions. payoff: plotting functions documented, testable usable plain R script (running app), module stays thin adapter. add change plot, put drawing logic exported interactive_* / static_* function module call . Gotcha: extracting render logic standalone function, preserve bindCache() scope. Several modules wrap plot-building reactive bindCache(...) keyed exactly inputs reads, re-rendering tab (another session viewing matrix settings) reuses result instead recomputing. move code reactive, make sure cache key still covers every input drawing now depends , otherwise either cache stale output defeat cache entirely.
code: output$sampleBoxplot <- renderPlotly({   interactive_boxplot(     selectmatrix_reactives$selectMatrix(),     selectmatrix_reactives$selectColData(),     groupby_reactives$getGroupby(),     expressiontype = selectmatrix_reactives$getAssayMeasure(),     palette = groupby_reactives$getPalette(),     hidden_groups = hiddenGroups(),     source = plot_source   ) %>%     shinyngsPlotlyConfig("boxplot", format = session$userData$plotFormat()) })

what: Adding a new module: a walkthrough
title: Developer guide: how modules work and how to add one
text: Say want myplot module. steps: 1. Create R/myplot.R trio. 2. Write standalone plot function. Put drawing logic exported interactive_myplot() (/static_myplot()) takes plain arguments. Give palette_name = COLORBLIND_PALETTE_NAME default produces consistent, colour-blind-safe output called outside app. 3. Add roxygen export right things. Document four functions roxygen blocks. Export standalone plotting function(s) @export; module trio follows package convention @keywords shiny generally kept internal (unexported), matching modules. Run devtools::document() regenerate NAMESPACE man/. 4. Wire app. prepare_app() simpleApp() locate module name (get(paste0(module, "Input")), get(module), etc.), correctly-named trio runnable standalone straight away: include larger app rnaseq, add myplotInput() / myplotOutput() app’s layout call myplot() server, alongside existing modules. 5. Add tests. Cover standalone plotting function directly (takes plain data, ’s easy test) add module-level tests alongside existing ones tests/. contributor conventions (naming, get*-reactive rule, running tests lints, document workflow) see CONTRIBUTING.md.
code: myplotInput <- function(id, eselist) {   ns <- NS(id)   expression_filters <- selectmatrixInput(ns("myplot"), eselist)   myplot_filters <- list(     # your own controls, each id wrapped in ns()     groupbyInput(ns("myplot"))   )   fieldSets(ns("fieldset"), list(settings = myplot_filters, expression = expression_filters)) }  myplotOutput <- function(id) {   ns <- NS(id)   moduleMain(     "My plot",     shinycssloaders::withSpinner(plotlyOutput(ns("myPlot")), color = shinyngsSpinnerColor()),     help = modalInput(ns(myplot_modal$id), "help", "help")   ) }  myplot <- function(id, eselist) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     selectmatrix_reactives <- selectmatrix("myplot", eselist)     groupby_reactives <- groupby("myplot", eselist = eselist, selectColData = selectmatrix_reactives$selectColData)      output$myPlot <- renderPlotly({       interactive_myplot(         selectmatrix_reactives$selectMatrix(),         selectmatrix_reactives$selectColData(),         groupby_reactives$getGroupby(),         palette = groupby_reactives$getPalette()       )     })   }) } app <- prepare_app("myplot", eselist) shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server)

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Panels getting first look structure, quality, clustering across samples. operate assay matrices sample metadata; require differential results.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Principal component analysis reduces high-dimensional data (tens thousands features) handful components, fixed linear combination original features. components plot plots first components one another separate samples strongest patterns data; colouring points sample variable (treatment, sex, sequencing lane) reveals factors drive structure. loading plot shows features contributed component, scree plot shows percentage variance explained component (plus cumulative total), helping judge many components worth examining. Inputs: assay matrix (variable rows selected default), choice components display, sample variable colour . Backed : compile_pca_data, points drawn via interactive_scatterplot scree curve via interactive_screeplot.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: companion PCA panel helps interpret components mean. analysis variance run component experimental factor, resulting matrix p-values drawn heatmap. Ideally key experimental variables associated first, highest-variance components; strong association technical factor (e.g. sequencing lane) early component flags variable account downstream. Inputs: assay matrix (variable rows default) set experimental variables test. Backed : interactive_pca_metadata_heatmap.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Computes matrix correlation values pairs samples draws heatmap accompanying dendrogram, alternative view sample relatedness dendrogram panel. Experimental annotations can added coloured bars heatmap via “Annotate variables” control. Inputs: assay matrix (variable rows default) optional annotation variables. Backed : interactive_heatmap.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Displays sample clustering dendrograms showing related samples one another. samples group expected, differential expression looking groups may present. Clustering controls let choose distance/similarity measure hierarchical clustering method, can colour samples experimental variable aid interpretation. Inputs: assay matrix (variable rows default; row selection may also “” supplied gene set) distance/clustering choices. Backed : interactive_clustering_dendrogram.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Illustrates distribution values sample, box plots (via ggplot2) lines/density (via Plotly). atypical distribution sample, example unusually low mean, can point sample-quality problems. Box plots become unmanageable beyond 20 samples, line plots default larger sample counts. Whiskers extend 1.5x interquartile range default; points beyond treated outliers, mousing outlier line plots reveals identity. Inputs: assay matrix; comparing raw versus normalised matrices can informative. Backed : interactive_boxplot, interactive_densityplot, interactive_quartiles.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: standard expression heatmap plotted interactively heatmaply. Clustering dendrograms use Spearman-correlation distance passed ward.D2 method hclust(). Colours scaled row default trends can compared across rows. data can optionally summarised means per sample group. Inputs: assay matrix, rows selectable variance, explicit list, (gene sets uploaded) predefined gene set. Backed : interactive_heatmap.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Examines clustering patterns within subset matrix. matrix re-scaled row give comparable profiles, chosen number clusters produced clara() method cluster package. number clusters key control, three display modes offered: sample 100 profiles per cluster, summary statistics (mean median) error bars filled variability band. per-cluster membership matrix can exported investigation. Inputs: assay matrix (variable rows default) number clusters. Backed : interactive_cluster_profiles.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Panels visualise tabulate results differential testing. require contrasts statistics (p- q-values) object’s tests slot; single assay carries tests, matrix selector shown. Fold changes computed dynamically chosen assay matrix way group-wise averages calculated (mean default).

title: Module and panel catalog
text: View download differential statistics: p-values adjusted p-values (computed prior loading) alongside fold changes (computed dynamically). Controls select contrasts display apply thresholds. Dynamic filters “Contrasts” allow complex queries, combining different filter sets across contrasts intersection (default) union, example “features highly significantly contrast unchanged contrast B”. Inputs: contrasts test statistics; assay matrix fold changes.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Plots log2 fold change (x, sign preserved) -log10 p-value (y), drawn Plotly. High-magnitude, low-variability changes land top-left top-right extremes, points interest. Points passing fold-change q-value thresholds drawn active blue mouse-detail; inert grey (labelling every point slow browser). Inputs: contrast, fold-change q-value thresholds, optionally set genes highlight colour. Backed : interactive_scatterplot.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Illustrates relationship mean expression fold change, overlaid significance statistical test. low expression small absolute difference yields large fold change falls within normal variability significant; expression rises, smaller fold changes become significant. threshold highlight controls volcano plot. Inputs: contrast, fold-change q-value thresholds, optional highlight set. Backed : interactive_scatterplot.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: simple scatter plot comparing mean expression value two groups samples every row matrix, drawn Plotly. Points grey, blue pass fold-change (available) q-value thresholds. Inputs: assay matrix, contrast, thresholds, optional highlight set. Backed : interactive_scatterplot.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: faceted boxplot differentially expressed genes chosen contrast: one panel per gene, samples grouped contrast’s two conditions, gene’s q-value annotated panel optional per-sample beeswarm overlay. Genes passing significance filters can ranked q-value (absolute) fold change ascending/descending, number drawn capped control. colour-blind-safe palette default. Inputs: contrast, significance thresholds, ranking metric, gene count, colour palette. Backed : interactive_topgene_boxplots.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: UpSet set-intersection plot (Lex, Gehlenborg et al.), sets gene lists derived supplied contrasts filters. can restrict number contrasts considered number intersections shown. intersection type defaults exclusive (Venn/Euler-style, bars sum total), can set “Complete” bar reflects full pairwise intersection (bars overlap longer sum total). plot downloadable. Inputs: contrasts thresholds, assay matrix generating comparisons. Backed : interactive_upset.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Panels centred individual genes gene sets.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Detailed information one genes selected label identifier. combines expression bar plot (expression per sample, optionally coloured experimental variable) gene info table annotation (links external resources configured), contrasts table listing gene’s differential results across defined contrasts, , experiment associated Ensembl species, gene model view: interactive genome-browser diagram gene’s exon/transcript structure. Inputs: one selected genes; assay matrix “colour ” variable bar plot; contrasts results table; Ensembl species (plus chromosome_name/start_position/end_position feature metadata) gene model. Backed : interactive_scatterplot expression bar plot; gene model view uses igvShiny (igv.js) browser.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: interactive barcode plot reproducing statistics behind limma’s barcodeplot() building Subramanian et al. (GSEA). shows members one gene set fall overall list genes ranked fold change two conditions; “bunching ” one extreme indicates coordinated - -regulation. FDR annotated available, hovering tick shows gene fold change, accompanying table gives contrast detail set. Inputs: single selected gene set, contrast, assay matrix (note displayed FDR may correspond one available matrices). Backed : interactive_barcodeplot.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: gene set analysis table, potentially produced variety methods (“Method” line names tool, e.g. GSEA ROAST, generated current results), typically showing gene sets p-values FDRs. Filters select single gene-set type (e.g. KEGG) optionally specific sets. convenience, significantly differential genes appropriate direction annotated set; default filter genes unadjusted p-value, since selection already applied set level. Gene sets link barcode plot, individual genes expression bar plots. Inputs: precomputed gene set analysis results, gene-set type, contrast gene-level thresholds.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Panels tied particular assay platform analysis method.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Plots mean Illumina control probes sample, grouped condition. Expected relationships check (listed alongside plot) include cy3_high > cy3_med > cy3_low, low stringency near zero, high stringency cy3 high, housekeeping biotin high, negative controls near zero. Inputs: Illumina control-probe measurements per sample. Backed : interactive_illumina_control_probes.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Visualises output DEXSeq package differential exon usage conditions. Per exon sample, DEXSeq considers ratio reads mapping exon versus exons gene, ratio changes across conditions. Individual sub-plots can toggled: model-derived expression per condition, exon usage coefficients, per-sample normalised counts, gene model colouring exons pink significant differential usage seen, transcripts view comparing usage known transcript structures. accompanying table gives normalised exon counts relative exon usage per condition. Inputs: DEXSeq results, gene plot, FDR threshold colouring exons, sub-plots show.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: tabular counterpart DEXSeq plot: normalised exon count relative exon usage per condition, large usage changes appear large fold changes low FDR-adjusted p-values. Controls filter fold change adjusted p-value; default single significant differential exon per gene shown (untick show ). Inputs: DEXSeq results fold-change/FDR filters.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Summaries read counts, drawn Plotly bar plots information supplied resource built. Example reports include read attrition plot (reads lost across analysis stages, drawn overlapping bars since stage subset previous), read distribution plot (reads genomic feature type exon intron), read gene-type plot (gene biotype, e.g. protein coding rRNA). Inputs: precomputed read-summary tables provided dataset. Backed : interactive_barchart / interactive_count_barplot.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Panels viewing downloading underlying data metadata.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: View download raw matrix data held assays slots. data organised experiments (set features, e.g. one transcripts one genes), experiment holds multiple assays (measurements features samples, different processing levels). data can optionally summarised means per sample group. Inputs: choice experiment assay, plus row column selection.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Shows experimental information supplied alongside primary data (used, example, batch correction define comparison groups); factors table colour plots elsewhere app. Category counts tab counts samples categorical field (e.g. per treatment group), optionally split second field grouped stacked bars. Identifier-like fields (near-unique per row) offered counting. Inputs: sample (column) metadata.

title: Module and panel catalog
text: Shows metadata associated matrix row (e.g. genes transcripts) selected experiment; content depends added analysis. sample table, Category counts tab counts rows categorical field (e.g. genes per biotype), optionally split second field, excluding identifier-like fields. title: Reusing components inside and outside Shiny

what: Calling a plot from a report
title: Reusing components inside and outside Shiny
text: minimal example: colour PCA scatter metadata variable, using compile_pca_data() coordinates make_color_scale() consistent palette. heatmap functions follow shape: pass matrix (, optionally, sample_annotation data frame) get widget back.
code: library(shinyngs)  pca <- compile_pca_data(my_matrix) groups <- my_metadata$treatment  interactive_scatterplot(   x = pca$coords[, "PC1"],   y = pca$coords[, "PC2"],   colorby = groups,   palette = make_color_scale(nlevels(factor(groups))),   xlab = "PC1",   ylab = "PC2" ) interactive_heatmap(   plotmatrix = my_matrix[select_variable_genes(my_matrix, ntop = 500), ],   sample_annotation = my_metadata["treatment"] )

what: Compute versus render: what shinyngs does and does not do
title: Reusing components inside and outside Shiny
text: shinyngs viewer, analysis engine. renders results already computed. handful exported helpers lightweight preparation plots need, fine use , statistics package: compile_pca_data() — principal components scatter/scree plots select_variable_genes() — pick variable features heatmap anova_pca_metadata() — associate PCs metadata association heatmap mad_score() — per-sample outlier scores cond_log2_transform_assays() — conditional log2 transform Differential statistics, enrichment testing similar analysis belong upstream (pipeline); pass outputs let shinyngs display .

title: Reusing components inside and outside Shiny
text: Every interactive plot rendered inside app carries download button honours app-wide PNG/SVG toggle, plot can exported vector SVG print. call plotly functions , apply configuration get named SVG download button:
code: p <- interactive_scatterplot(x, y, colorby = groups) plotly::config(   p,   toImageButtonOptions = list(format = "svg", filename = "pca") )

title: Getting started with shinyngs
text: quickest path wrap existing SummarizedExperiment ExploratorySummarizedExperiment, collect ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList (represents whole study), hand prepare_app(). prepare_app() produces ui server pass Shiny. Single-panel apps built way, e.g. prepare_app("heatmap", eselist) prepare_app("pca", eselist). zhangneurons dataset gives fuller, pre-configured example also carries differential results gene sets:
code: library(shinyngs)  data(airway, package = "airway") ese <- as(airway, "ExploratorySummarizedExperiment") eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(   ese,   title = "airway",   group_vars = c("cell", "dex") )  app <- prepare_app("rnaseq", eselist) shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) devtools::install_github("pinin4fjords/zhangneurons") data("zhangneurons")  app <- prepare_app("rnaseq", zhangneurons) shiny::shinyApp(app$ui, app$server)

title: Theming, palettes and shareable views
text: Every categorical colour shinyngs comes, default, single fixed palette: COLORBLIND_PALETTE, Okabe & Ito (2008) colour-blind-safe set eight hues, ordered colour-vision-deficiency separation stays highest neighbouring entries. original palette’s black swatch replaced mid grey (#595959) remains visible light dark app backgrounds. Colours handed exported make_color_scale() helper. draws fixed palette order, position always gets colour, group keeps colour across every plot app: Passing value treats RColorBrewer qualitative palette name (example "Dark2" "Set1"), expanded interpolation colours requested palette holds:
code: COLORBLIND_PALETTE #> "#E69F00" "#56B4E9" "#009E73" "#F0E442" #> "#0072B2" "#D55E00" "#CC79A7" "#595959" library(shinyngs)  # The colour-blind-safe palette is the default (palette = "colorblind") make_color_scale(4) #> "#E69F00" "#56B4E9" "#009E73" "#F0E442"  # Ask for more colours than the base palette provides and shades are # interpolated between them, with a message noting that colour-blind # separation can no longer be guaranteed for every pair. make_color_scale(12) make_color_scale(6, palette = "Dark2")

title: Theming, palettes and shareable views
text: Modules colour experimental variable expose palette drop-colormaker module (colormakerInput() / colormaker()). first choice, labelled “Colour-blind safe”, selects COLORBLIND_PALETTE; remaining choices RColorBrewer qualitative palettes, offered users prefer . colour-blind-safe palette always pre-selected default. practice rarely wire colormaker : groupby module already embeds picker via colormakerInput() returns getPalette (reactive) alongside getGroupby. plotting module passes palette straight standalone plot function (example interactive_boxplot(..., palette = groupby_reactives$getPalette())). plot functions also accept palette_name argument (defaulting colour-blind-safe palette) produce consistent colours called directly, outside app.
code: # UI: the palette drop-down colormakerInput(ns("groupby"))  # Server: a reactive returning the chosen palette, sized to the number of # categories the caller reports getPalette <- colormaker("groupby", getNumberCategories = getNumberCategories)

title: Theming, palettes and shareable views
text: navbar carries light/dark switch built bslib::input_dark_mode(), fixed id shinyngs_dark_mode. Toggling flips Bootstrap’s data-bs-theme attribute, restyles whole page. One subtlety worth knowing: input_dark_mode sets data-bs-theme web component hydrates client. avoid flash light theme dark-resolved load, shinyngsPageNavbar() injects tiny inline <head> script resolves OS colour scheme sets data-bs-theme first paint. dark-mode toggle deliberately bookmarked (see ), shared link force colour-scheme preference whoever opens .

title: Theming, palettes and shareable views
text: Theming centralised shinyngsPageNavbar() rather exposed per-app option, app types (rnaseq, chipseq, illuminaarray, single-module simpleApp) get look. re-skin app, change made one place: swap bootswatch name primary colour bs_theme() call, CSS plots follow Bootstrap variables produces.

title: Theming, palettes and shareable views
text: shinyngs uses Shiny’s URL bookmarking (enableBookmarking("url")) current view, , tab open, experiment assay selected, row/column filters, plot options , encoded address bar. Copying URL re-opening restores view. Bookmarking enabled two places: prepare_app() sets option present wherever app object ends , configureBookmarking() sets running session (harnesses can transport app object fresh process earlier option lost).

title: Theming, palettes and shareable views
text: navbar’s “Share view” control actionButton id shinyngs_share_view. Clicking calls session$doBookmark(), writes state URL address bar, copies clipboard (via custom shinyngs_copy_link message handled package JS), shows confirmation notification.
code: observeEvent(input$shinyngs_share_view, {   session$doBookmark() })  onBookmarked(function(url) {   updateQueryString(url)   session$sendCustomMessage("shinyngs_copy_link", url)   showNotification("Shareable link copied to your clipboard ...") })

title: Theming, palettes and shareable views
text: configureBookmarking() keeps live exclude list URL stays useful. Transient bloating inputs excluded appear: DataTable internals (_rows_current, _state, _search, …), plotly event streams (plotly_), help-modal triggers, presentation controls shouldn’t travel shared link (shinyngs_dark_mode, shinyngs_plot_format, shinyngs_share_view). observer watches newly-appeared inputs (tables inserted contrast filters stream initial page) pushes updated exclude set via setBookmarkExclude(). Two pieces state need explicit handling bslib navsets server-side selectize inputs don’t bookmark : active navbar tab saved onBookmark() re-applied onRestored() via updateTabsetPanel(). Server-side selectize inputs hold options client-side, saved value re-applied choices repopulate. bookmarkedInputValue() reads value back restore state, trying module’s namespaced id first falling back bare id. net effect: colleague opening shared link lands tab, looking experiment, assay, filters plot settings , light/dark preference left untouched.
code: bookmarkedInputValue(state, session, "myField")

title: Shiny apps for NGS data
text: Shinyngs differs START similar applications (see also Degust), effort made provide analysis capabilities. envisaged process : RNA-seq data analysed, producing set matrices, p/q values generated given set comparisons. Matrix comparison data loaded modified SummarizedExperiment structure provided Shinyngs, serialised. easily automated. Serialised object used input autmoatically produce Shiny app using Shinyngs. great many experimental designs analysis methods, building Shinyngs ’ve taken view analysis best left analyst. envisaged use case bioinformatician attempting convey results analysis non-experts. ShinyNGS provides number capabilities may find applications: Simple selection gene sets name/ annotation modify plots tables shown. Progressive filters differential analysis: “Show genes differential contrasts contrasts” Large variety visualisations: row-wise clustering, UpSet-style intersection plots, gene set enrichment barcode plots etc. interactive igv.js-powered gene model view gene page, showing exon/transcript structure alongside expression, feature metadata includes genomic coordinates Ensembl species configured (see --ensembl_species ). “Share view” button captures current selections page URL, configured view can bookmarked shared link.

title: ExploratorySummarizedExperiments — ExploratorySummarizedExperiment
text: assays object class SimpleList supplied SummarizedExperiment constructor colData object class DataFrame supplied SummarizedExperimentConstructor. Row names correspond column names matrices assays annotation data frame annotation features (rows) assays matrices. Rows must correspond matrices. idfield annotation columns row names correspond? labelfield column annotation used label features (e.g. gene name field)? entrezgenefield column annotation Entrez gene ID? contrast_stats List matrices containing contrast-related statistics. 'pvals', 'qvals' 'fold_changes' currently used. Fold changes calculated fly supplied. Matrix columns correspond 'contrasts' set containing SummarizedExperimentList. assay_measures Optional List measures display related assay. gene_set_analyses Three-level nested lists gene set tables keyed first assay, gene set type contrast. dexseq_results optional list DEXSeqResults objects corresponding contrasts listed contrasts slot.. read_reports named list matrices read counts columns sample names rows. Useful providing mapped read counts, counts per gene type etc gene_set_analyses_tool Three-level nested lists string, nested gene_set_analyses. string may "auto" (default), "gsea" "roast". defines format corresponding gene_set_analyses table.

text: eses List ExploratorySummarizedExperiments title Study title author Study authors description Study summary displayed front page static_pdf URL static PDF document displayed front page. group_vars Variables user allowed group samples individual experiments, must correspond colData default_groupvar Default group_var contrasts List length-3 vectors containing 1) group_var, 2) group_var value corresponding 'control' side 3) value corresponding 'treatment' side url_roots list URL roots, list names corresponding metadata column names experiments. Exploratory tools displayed via shinyngs can use roots construct URLs 'link '. gene_sets list named lists character vectors gene identifiers, produced reading .gmt format gene sets (example MSigDB) read_gmt. must contain identifiers type specified gene_set_id_type. gene_set_id_type column found metadata component ExploratorySummarizedExperiment objects via mcols(). Used relate rows assays gene sets. ensembl_species Ensembl species definition like 'mmusculus'. Used pick igv.js genome build gene model view gene module (see geneModelGenomeInfo()).

Some have a # different value for every sample, one has the same value for all of them.  colData(airway) #> DataFrame with 8 rows and 9 columns #>            SampleName     cell      dex    albut        Run avgLength #>              <factor> <factor> <factor> <factor>   <factor> <integer> #> SRR1039508 GSM1275862  N61311     untrt    untrt SRR1039508       126 #> SRR1039509 GSM1275863  N61311     trt      untrt SRR1039509       126 #> SRR1039512 GSM1275866  N052611    untrt    untrt SRR1039512       126 #> SRR1039513 GSM1275867  N052611    trt      untrt SRR1039513        87 #> SRR1039516 GSM1275870  N080611    untrt    untrt SRR1039516       120 #> SRR1039517 GSM1275871  N080611    trt      untrt SRR1039517       126 #> SRR1039520 GSM1275874  N061011    untrt    untrt SRR1039520       101 #> SRR1039521 GSM1275875  N061011    trt      untrt SRR1039521        98 #>            Experiment    Sample    BioSample #>              <factor>  <factor>     <factor> #> SRR1039508  SRX384345 SRS508568 SAMN02422669 #> SRR1039509  SRX384346 SRS508567 SAMN02422675 #> SRR1039512  SRX384349 SRS508571 SAMN02422678 #> SRR1039513  SRX384350 SRS508572 SAMN02422670 #> SRR1039516  SRX384353 SRS508575 SAMN02422682 #> SRR1039517  SRX384354 SRS508576 SAMN02422673 #> SRR1039520  SRX384357 SRS508579 SAMN02422683 #> SRR1039521  SRX384358 SRS508580 SAMN02422677  # So just pick the variables that ARE useful  choose_grouping_variables(data.frame(colData(airway))) #> [1] "cell" "dex"

text: plotmatrix Expression/ data matrix experiment Annotation columns plotmatrix colorby Column name experiment specifying boxes colored cor_method Correlation method, passed cor() (default: pearson). cluster_method Clustering method, passed hclust() (default: ward.D). plot_title Plot title labelspace Vertical fraction plot used labels (default: 0.2). palette Palette colors, one unique value derived colorby. palette_name Valid R color palette name

title: Make a clustering dendrogram with coloring by experimental variable — clustering_dendrogram
code: # Make a dendrogram with the data in airway  require(airway) data(airway, package = "airway") clustering_dendrogram(assays(airway)[[1]], data.frame(colData(airway)), colorby = "dex")   # Do the same, but only usig the 1000 most variant rows and see how the # clustering improves.  mymatrix <- assays(airway)[[1]] mymatrix <- mymatrix[order(apply(mymatrix, 1, var), decreasing = TRUE)[1:1000], ] clustering_dendrogram(mymatrix, data.frame(colData(airway)), colorby = "dex")

title: Conditionally apply log2 transformation on assay data based on log2_assays parameter. — cond_log2_transform_assays
text: assay_data list containing matrices assay data. log2_assays string parameter can NULL, empty, non-empty string. NULL: log2 transformation guessed based input assays. empty: log2 transformation applied. non-empty: log2 transformation applied unconditionally specified assays. threshold numeric threshold determine matrix log-transformed. checked should_transform NULL. reverse Boolean, unlog rather log? prettify_names Boolean. Prettify element names? Passed validate_indices(). invert_assays Boolean, apply transform assays specified log2_assays.

text: matrix_data matrix containing data. should_transform boolean indicating log2 transformation applied. TRUE, log2 transformation applied unconditionally. FALSE, transformation applied. code: expression <- matrix(1:12, nrow = 3,   dimnames = list(c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3"), paste0("s", 1:4))) coldata <- data.frame(   condition = rep(c("treated", "control"), each = 2),   row.names = paste0("s", 1:4) ) annotation <- data.frame(   gene_id = c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3"),   gene_name = c("GeneA", "GeneB", "GeneC"),   row.names = c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3") ) ese <- ExploratorySummarizedExperiment(   assays = list(expression = expression),   colData = coldata,   annotation = annotation,   idfield = "gene_id",   labelfield = "gene_name" ) convert_ids(c("ENSG1", "ENSG2"), ese, "gene_name") #> [1] "GeneA" "GeneB"

title: Build an ExploratorySummarisedExperimentList from a description provided in a list — eselist_from_config
code: sample_metadata_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(sample = paste0("s", 1:4), condition = rep(c("treated", "control"), each = 2)),   sample_metadata_file, row.names = FALSE ) feature_metadata_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(gene_id = c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3"), gene_name = c("GeneA", "GeneB", "GeneC")),   feature_metadata_file, row.names = FALSE ) mat <- matrix(1:12, nrow = 3, dimnames = list(c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3"), paste0("s", 1:4))) matrix_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(gene_id = rownames(mat), mat, check.names = FALSE),   matrix_file, row.names = FALSE ) differential_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(     gene_id = rownames(mat), log2FoldChange = c(1.2, -0.5, 2.1),     pvalue = c(0.01, 0.2, 0.001), padj = c(0.02, 0.3, 0.005)   ),   differential_file, row.names = FALSE )  config <- list(   title = "Example study",   author = "An Author",   experiments = list(     rnaseq = list(       coldata = list(file = sample_metadata_file, id = "sample"),       annotation = list(file = feature_metadata_file, id = "gene_id", label = "gene_name"),       expression_matrices = list(expression = list(file = matrix_file, measure = "Counts"))     )   ),   contrasts = list(     comparisons = list(c("condition", "treated", "control")),     stats = list(       rnaseq = list(         expression = list(           type = "uncompiled",           files = list(differential_file),           feature_id_column = "gene_id",           fc_column = "log2FoldChange",           pval_column = "pvalue",           qval_column = "padj",           fold_change_scale = "log2"         )       )     )   ) ) eselist_from_config(config, log2_assays = "") #> Constructing text: simple study one 'experiement' Gene-level results, three 'assays' describing raw, filtered normalised expression might make YAML like:
code: title: My RNA seq experiment author: Joe Blogs report: report.md group_vars:   - Group   - Replicate default_groupvar: Group experiments:   Gene:     coldata:       file: my.experiment.csv       id: External     annotation:       file: my.annotation.csv       id: gene_id       entrez: ~       label: gene_id     expression_matrices:       Raw:         file: raw_counts.csv         measure: counts       Filtered:         file: filtered_counts.csv         measure: Counts per million       Normalised:         file: normalised_counts.csv         measure: Counts per million     read_reports:       read_attrition: read_attrition.csv contrasts:   comparisons:   - Variable: Group     Group.1: control     Group.2: TreatmentA   - Variable: Group     Group.1: control     Group.2: TreatmentB contrast_stats:   Gene:     Normalised:       pvals: pvals.csv       qvals: qvals.csv

what: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
text: Shinyngs stores fold changes internally signed linear values (ratio two conditions, absolute magnitude least 1 sign indicating direction - see fold_change), value strictly -1 1 possible log2 scale (e.g. log2 fold change 0.5 ~1.4-fold change). log2 fold changes also typically small symmetric around zero, linear fold changes can large magnitudes effect size (e.g. 1000-fold change log2 fold change ~10).

title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
code: guess_foldchange_scale(values, log2_magnitude_limit = 15)

title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
text: values Numeric vector fold change values (NAs non-finite values ignored). log2_magnitude_limit Values absolute magnitude greater considered implausible log2 fold change (default 15, corresponding ~32,000-fold change).

title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
text: One "log2", "linear" "ambiguous".

title: Guess whether fold change values are on a log2 or linear scale — guess_foldchange_scale
code: guess_foldchange_scale(c(-2.1, 0.3, 1.8, -0.05)) #> [1] "log2" guess_foldchange_scale(c(-8, 2, 45, -120)) #> [1] "linear" guess_foldchange_scale(c(-2, 3, 5, -8)) #> [1] "ambiguous"

code: expression <- matrix(1:12, nrow = 3,   dimnames = list(c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3"), paste0("s", 1:4))) coldata <- data.frame(   condition = rep(c("treated", "control"), each = 2),   row.names = paste0("s", 1:4) ) annotation <- data.frame(   gene_id = c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3"),   gene_name = c("GeneA", "GeneB", "GeneC"),   row.names = c("ENSG1", "ENSG2", "ENSG3") ) ese <- ExploratorySummarizedExperiment(   assays = list(expression = expression),   colData = coldata,   annotation = annotation,   idfield = "gene_id",   labelfield = "gene_name" ) id_to_label(c("ENSG1", "ENSG2"), ese) #> [1] "GeneA / ENSG1" "GeneB / ENSG2"

what: Make an interactive boxplot with coloring by experimental variable — interactive_boxplot
title: Make an interactive boxplot with coloring by experimental variable — interactive_boxplot
text: Draws plotly box plot value distribution sample. Box statistics (quartiles, whiskers, outliers) computed server-side supplied plotly precomputed values, browser payload scales number samples rather size matrix. makes usable full expression matrices sending every point .

title: Make an interactive boxplot with coloring by experimental variable — interactive_boxplot
code: interactive_boxplot(   plotmatrices,   experiment,   colorby = NULL,   palette = NULL,   expressiontype = "expression",   palette_name = COLORBLIND_PALETTE_NAME,   whisker_distance = 1.5,   annotate_samples = FALSE,   should_transform = NULL,   max_outliers = 500,   hidden_groups = character(0),   source = NULL )

title: Make an interactive boxplot with coloring by experimental variable — interactive_boxplot
text: plotmatrices Expression/ data matrix, named list thereof experiment Annotation columns plotmatrix colorby Column name experiment specifying boxes colored palette Palette colors, one unique value derived colorby. expressiontype Expression type use y axis label palette_name Valid R color palette name whisker_distance IQR multiplier whiskers, boundary beyond points drawn outliers (see coef geom_boxplot, default: 1.5) annotate_samples Add suffix sample labels reflecting group? should_transform boolean indicating log2 transformation applied. TRUE, log2 transformation applied unconditionally. FALSE, transformation applied. NULL, conditional transformation based threshold applied. max_outliers Maximum number outlier points draw per facet. sample set produces , extreme (furthest median) kept overlay stays bounded (default: 500). hidden_groups Values colorby exclude: samples dropped plot redrawn remainder, stay legend (legendonly) can toggled back . source Optional plotly source id used route legend-click (plotly_restyle) events back Shiny session.

what: Plot expression profiles for a set of feature clusters with plot_ly() — interactive_cluster_profiles
title: Plot expression profiles for a set of feature clusters with plot_ly() — interactive_cluster_profiles
text: Draws one sub-plot per cluster scaled expression matrix (e.g. produced run_clustering), combined plotly::subplot(). Three display modes available: individual sample lines, mean/median line error bars, mean/median line shaded error band. Summary statistics (mean, median spreads) derived internally summary_se.

title: Plot expression profiles for a set of feature clusters with plot_ly() — interactive_cluster_profiles
code: interactive_cluster_profiles(   matrices_by_cluster,   cluster_display = c("filled_line", "sample_lines", "error_bars"),   average_type = c("mean", "median"),   limits = c("sd", "se", "ci"),   colors = NULL,   sample_order = NULL,   max_sample_lines = 100,   summarised_matrices_by_cluster = NULL )

title: Plot expression profiles for a set of feature clusters with plot_ly() — interactive_cluster_profiles
text: matrices_by_cluster named list matrices/data frames, one per cluster, features row samples column (e.g. input matrix split clustering$clustering run_clustering). Names cluster numbers produced split(), since colors indexed cluster number. cluster_display 'filled_line' (mean/median line shaded error band), 'sample_lines' (individual sample profiles) 'error_bars' (mean/median line error bars) average_type 'mean' 'median' limits spread show around average: 'sd' (standard deviation), 'se' (standard error) 'ci' (95% confidence interval). Ignored cluster_display = "sample_lines" colors vector colors, indexed cluster number (.e. colors[[2]] colors cluster named "2" matrices_by_cluster). Defaults make_color_scale sample_order Character vector giving sample (x axis) display order. Defaults colnames() first cluster's matrix max_sample_lines Maximum number sample lines drawn per cluster cluster_display = "sample_lines"; larger clusters randomly subsampled many rows summarised_matrices_by_cluster Precomputed summary statistics, one element per cluster, returned summary_se element matrices_by_cluster. Computed internally supplied; callers already (e.g. cache separately display controls ) can pass directly

title: Plot expression profiles for a set of feature clusters with plot_ly() — interactive_cluster_profiles

title: Make an interactive clustering dendrogram colored by experimental variable — interactive_clustering_dendrogram
text: plotly counterpart clustering_dendrogram(): branch geometry derived hclust() tree, leaves placed markers colored experimental variable. Sample names sit x-axis (rotated, plotly reserving label margin), hovering leaf reveals sample name group value.

title: Make an interactive clustering dendrogram colored by experimental variable — interactive_clustering_dendrogram
text: plotmatrix Expression/ data matrix experiment Annotation columns plotmatrix colorby Column name experiment specifying leaves colored cor_method Correlation method, passed cor() (default: pearson). cluster_method Clustering method, passed hclust() (default: ward.D). plot_title Plot title palette Palette colors, one unique value derived colorby. palette_name Valid R color palette name hidden_groups Values colorby exclude: samples dropped tree recomputed remainder, groups stay legend (legendonly) can toggled back . source plotly event source string, used route legend-click (plotly_restyle) events back Shiny session.

what: Make an Illumina microarray control-probe QC plot with plot_ly() — interactive_illumina_control_probes
title: Make an Illumina microarray control-probe QC plot with plot_ly() — interactive_illumina_control_probes
text: Averages fixed set Illumina control probe groups (cy3 dilution series, stringency, negative, biotin, labeling housekeeping controls) across samples plots lines, following standard QC relationships groups (see illuminaarrayqcInput's displayed checklist: cy3_high > cy3_med > cy3_low, low stringency =~ 0, high stringency <= cy3 high, housekeeping/biotin = high, negative =~ 0).

what: Combine the PCA-vs-metadata heatmap with a synced scree plot — interactive_pca_variance_heatmap
title: Combine the PCA-vs-metadata heatmap with a synced scree plot — interactive_pca_variance_heatmap
text: Stacks interactive_screeplot directly interactive_pca_metadata_heatmap single figure, sharing one x-axis. plots use identical, identically-ordered PC labels x-categories, lines scree point corresponding heatmap column, letting "PC significantly associated" cell read alongside much variance PC actually explains.

title: Combine the PCA-vs-metadata heatmap with a synced scree plot — interactive_pca_variance_heatmap
code: interactive_pca_variance_heatmap(   pca_coords,   pcameta,   fraction_explained,   cluster_rows = TRUE,   n_components = 10,   heatmap_height = 600,   scree_height = 200,   ... )

title: Combine the PCA-vs-metadata heatmap with a synced scree plot — interactive_pca_variance_heatmap
text: pca_coords Data frame PCA coordinates, samples row components column (e.g. x element runPCA's prcomp result). pcameta Data frame sample metadata sample identifiers row variables column. fraction_explained Numeric vector containing percent contribution variance component (e.g. calculatePCAFractionExplained). cluster_rows Cluster variables (rows) p value profile across components? n_components Number leading components show/test, passed interactive_screeplot interactive_pca_metadata_heatmap. heatmap_height rendered height, pixels, heatmap portion combined figure. scree_height rendered height, pixels, scree portion combined figure. ... Additional arguments passed interactive_pca_metadata_heatmap

what: Make a line-based alternative to boxplots — interactive_quartiles
title: Make a line-based alternative to boxplots — interactive_quartiles
text: Box-plots become unmanagable large numbers samples. function plots lines median, quartiles, whiskers, plotting points outliers beyond

title: Make a line-based alternative to boxplots — interactive_quartiles
code: interactive_quartiles(   matrix,   labels = rownames(matrix),   expressiontype = "expression",   whisker_distance = 1.5,   should_transform = NULL )

title: Make a line-based alternative to boxplots — interactive_quartiles
text: matrix Numeric matrix labels String vector labels used matrix row expressiontype Y axis label whisker_distance IQR multiplier whiskers, beyond show outliers (see coef geom_boxplot) should_transform boolean indicating log2 transformation applied. TRUE, log2 transformation applied unconditionally. FALSE, transformation applied. NULL, conditional transformation based threshold applied.

title: Make a line-based alternative to boxplots — interactive_quartiles
text: plotly htmlwidget

title: Make a line-based alternative to boxplots — interactive_quartiles
code: data(airway, package = "airway") interactive_quartiles(assays(airway)[[1]], as(airway, "ExploratorySummarizedExperiment")) #> Error in as.vector(x): no method for coercing this S4 class to a vector

title: Make scatterplots with plot_ly() — interactive_scatterplot
text: Make scatterplots plot_ly()

title: Make scatterplots with plot_ly() — interactive_scatterplot
code: interactive_scatterplot(   x,   y,   z = NULL,   colorby = NULL,   plot_type = "scatter",   title = "",   legend_title = "",   xlab = "x",   ylab = "y",   zlab = "z",   palette = NULL,   point_size = 5,   labels = NULL,   show_labels = FALSE,   lines = NULL,   hline_thresholds = NULL,   vline_thresholds = NULL,   xrange = NULL,   yrange = NULL,   showlegend = TRUE,   palette_name = COLORBLIND_PALETTE_NAME,   colorby_menu = NULL )

title: Make scatterplots with plot_ly() — interactive_scatterplot
text: x X coordinates y Y coordinates z Optional Z coordinates colorby String vector factor specifying value groups plot_type Plot type: 'scatter' 'scatter3d' title Plot title legend_title Legend title xlab X label ylab Y label zlab Z label palette Color palette correct number groups 'colorby' point_size Main point size labels Point labels show_labels Permanently show labels labelled points (default just hoverover). Ignored colorby_menu supplied. lines 3 column data-frame (name, x, y) two rows, one start end named line hline_thresholds Named list horizontal lines y coordinates vline_thresholds Named list vertical lines x coordinates xrange Optional fixed c(min, max) x axis range, e.g. keep volcano plot symmetric around zero. applied lines/thresholds drawn; otherwise plotly's autorange used. Defaults NULL (derive range data). yrange Optional fixed c(min, max) y axis range. caveats xrange. showlegend Boolean: show legend? palette_name Valid R color palette name colorby_menu Opt-, named list alternative colour vectors (one per entry, length x/y(/z)). supplied, plot coloured using addColorbyMenu instead colorby, plotly dropdown added letting reader switch variable colours points; colorby shown unless also included entry colorby_menu. intended standalone/static-HTML report use : Shiny scatterplot modules already offer colour-selectInput, must set argument, since always--widget dropdown alongside control give readers two competing ways change colouring. Default NULL ().

what: Make a PCA scree plot with plot_ly() — interactive_screeplot
title: Make a PCA scree plot with plot_ly() — interactive_screeplot
text: Plots percent variance explained principal component lines+markers trace, optional cumulative variance overlay.

title: Make a PCA scree plot with plot_ly() — interactive_screeplot
code: interactive_screeplot(   fraction_explained,   n_components = NULL,   cumulative = FALSE,   palette_name = COLORBLIND_PALETTE_NAME,   title = "Scree plot" )

title: Make a PCA scree plot with plot_ly() — interactive_screeplot
text: fraction_explained Numeric vector percent variance explained principal component, order (e.g. calculatePCAFractionExplained, percentVar element returned compile_pca_data). n_components Number leading components plot. Defaults fraction_explained. cumulative Boolean: add cumulative variance explained trace? palette_name Valid R color palette name title Plot title

title: Make a PCA scree plot with plot_ly() — interactive_screeplot
