what: Make an interactive faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — interactive_topgene_boxplots
title: Make an interactive faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — interactive_topgene_boxplots
text: plotly equivalent static_topgene_boxplots. One subplot drawn per gene, samples grouped condition. beeswarm overlay approximated using plotly's native jittered box points, since (typically ) per-sample values drawn shipping ggbeeswarm's point layout browser necessary .

title: Make an interactive faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — interactive_topgene_boxplots
code: interactive_topgene_boxplots(   assay,   groupby,   genes,   annotations = NULL,   labels = NULL,   beeswarm = TRUE,   ncol = NULL,   palette = NULL,   palette_name = COLORBLIND_PALETTE_NAME,   expressiontype = "expression",   should_transform = NULL )

title: Make an interactive faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — interactive_topgene_boxplots
text: assay Numeric matrix, genes (rows) samples (columns) groupby Vector group labels, one per column assay genes Character vector row names assay facet , order facets appear. Also used look values assay, must match row names even labels supplied. annotations Optional named character vector keyed values genes, rendered per-facet annotation (e.g. q value string) labels Optional named character vector keyed values genes, used facet title place raw gene identifier (e.g. gene symbol genes holds Ensembl IDs). Genes missing labels fall back raw identifier. genes still drives lookup assay matching annotations. beeswarm Overlay individual points using geom_quasirandom? ncol Number facet columns. Defaults min(3, length(genes)) palette Palette colours, one unique value groupby palette_name Valid R color palette name expressiontype Expression type use y axis label should_transform boolean indicating log2 transformation applied. TRUE, log2 transformation applied unconditionally. FALSE, transformation applied. NULL, conditional transformation based threshold applied.

title: Make an interactive faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — interactive_topgene_boxplots
text: output plotly output

title: Make an interactive faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — interactive_topgene_boxplots

what: Make an UpSet-style set intersection plot with plot_ly() — interactive_upset
title: Make an UpSet-style set intersection plot with plot_ly() — interactive_upset
text: reimplementation upset tool Lex, Gehlenborg et al: bar chart set sizes, bar chart intersection sizes, grid showing sets make intersection - drawn plotly rather base graphics stays interactive outside fixed set count, elements given intersection can plotted (rather assigning every item highest-order intersection).

title: Make an UpSet-style set intersection plot with plot_ly() — interactive_upset
code: interactive_upset(   sets,   nintersects = 20,   minorder = 1,   set_sort = TRUE,   bar_numbers = FALSE,   show_empty_intersections = TRUE,   intersection_assignment_type = c("upset", "all") )

title: Make an UpSet-style set intersection plot with plot_ly() — interactive_upset
text: sets named list character vectors, one per set (e.g. /differential gene sets per contrast) nintersects Maximum number intersections display, ordered size minorder Minimum number sets must involved intersection shown set_sort Sort sets size (ascending) plotting? bar_numbers Add value labels intersection size bars? show_empty_intersections Include intersections/set combinations zero members? intersection_assignment_type 'upset' assigns member highest-order intersection (original UpSet); '' counts member every intersection belongs

title: Make an UpSet-style set intersection plot with plot_ly() — interactive_upset
text: output plotly htmlwidget

title: Make an UpSet-style set intersection plot with plot_ly() — interactive_upset
text: Lex Gehlenborg (2014). Points view: Sets intersections. Nature Methods 11, 779 (2014). http://www.nature.com/nmeth/journal/v11/n8/abs/nmeth.3033.html Gehlenborg N (2016). UpSetR: Scalable Alternative Venn Euler Diagrams Visualizing Intersecting Sets. R package version 1.3.0, https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=UpSetR

title: Calculate MAD scores as per OmicSoft — mad_score
text: matrix Matrix samples column sample_sheet Sample sheet samples row groupby Sample sheet column can used group samples outlier_threshold Value points flagged outliers, conventionally -5

title: Calculate MAD scores as per OmicSoft — mad_score

title: Calculate MAD scores as per OmicSoft — mad_score
text: currently deployed anywhere shinyngs, potential way flagging outliers investigation

title: Calculate MAD scores as per OmicSoft — mad_score
code: mat <- matrix(rnorm(60), nrow = 6, ncol = 10,   dimnames = list(paste0("gene", 1:6), paste0("s", 1:10))) sample_sheet <- data.frame(   condition = rep(c("treated", "control"), each = 5),   row.names = colnames(mat) ) mad_score(mat, sample_sheet, groupby = "condition") #>       group        mad outlier #> s6  control  0.6744908   FALSE #> s7  control  0.1248481   FALSE #> s8  control -7.3259914    TRUE #> s9  control -1.0314661   FALSE #> s10 control  0.0000000   FALSE #> s1  treated  0.0000000   FALSE #> s2  treated -0.2072914   FALSE #> s3  treated  0.6744908   FALSE #> s4  treated  0.9354219   FALSE #> s5  treated -2.2211295   FALSE

title: Make UI and server functions for Shiny apps based on data supplied as modfied SummarizedExperiments — prepare_app
code: prepare_app(type, eselist, ui_only = FALSE, ...)

title: Make UI and server functions for Shiny apps based on data supplied as modfied SummarizedExperiments — prepare_app
text: type string specifying type shiny app required. Currently, 'rnaseq' 'chipseq' produce large multi-panel applications designed facilitate analysis data types. 'type', call passed simpleApp(), attempt build application using Input Output functions module. eselist ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList object containing assay data (expression, counts...), sample data annotation data rows. ui_only add server components (UI testing) ... Additional arguments passed simpleApp()

title: Make UI and server functions for Shiny apps based on data supplied as modfied SummarizedExperiments — prepare_app
text: output list length 2 containing: UI server components

title: Make UI and server functions for Shiny apps based on data supplied as modfied SummarizedExperiments — prepare_app
code: library(shinyngs)  # 1: BASIC RNA-SEQ APP  # Get an example RNA-seq dataset from the `airway` package  data(airway, package = "airway")  # Get some information about these data from the package description  expinfo <- packageDescription("airway")  # Convert to an ExploratorySummarizedExperiment (with extra slots)  ese <- as(airway, "ExploratorySummarizedExperiment")  # Make the ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList that represents the study as a # whole.  eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(   ese,   title = expinfo$Title,   author = expinfo$Author,   description = expinfo$Description ) #> Creating ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList object  # Make and run the app  if (interactive()) {   app <- prepare_app("rnaseq", eselist)   shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) }  # 2: AUGMENT WITH ANNOTATION INFO FOR MORE INFORMATIVE APP  # Add row metadata (gene symbol, genomic coordinates) so plots and tables # are labelled meaningfully, then choose a different default grouping # variable and re-make the app. Any source of per-gene annotation works # here (a GTF, an annotation package, a pre-built CSV); the requirement is # a data.frame keyed by the same IDs as rownames(ese). Adding # chromosome_name/start_position/end_position columns plus ensembl_species # also enables the igv.js gene model view on the gene page.  if (interactive()) {   annotation <- data.frame(     ensembl_gene_id = rownames(ese),     external_gene_name = rownames(ese), # substitute real gene symbols     chromosome_name = "1", # substitute real coordinates     start_position = seq_along(rownames(ese)),     end_position = seq_along(rownames(ese)) + 1000   )    mcols(ese) <- annotation[match(rownames(ese), annotation$ensembl_gene_id), ]   ese@labelfield <- "external_gene_name"    eselist <- ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList(     ese,     title = expinfo$Title,     author = expinfo$Author,     description = expinfo$Description,     default_groupvar = "dex",     ensembl_species = "hsapiens" # enables the igv.js gene model view   )   app <- prepare_app("rnaseq", eselist)   shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server) }  # 3. MORE COMPLEX DATA FOR DIFFERENTIAL EXPRESSION ETC  # See the vignette for how to populate the extra slots of an # ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList (contrasts, differential statistics, # gene set analyses etc.). With those in place the resulting app gains # additional panels for differential analyses.

what: Read and validate a contrasts file against sample metadata — read_contrasts
title: Read and validate a contrasts file against sample metadata — read_contrasts
text: Checks: 1. duplicate contrast IDs. Ensure required columns (variable, reference, target) present. 2. Values contrast variable column exist column names sample metadata. 3. blocking factors supplied, checks present sample metadata. 4. Design matrix full rank. 5. Warn continuous covariates (e.g. numeric patient IDs treated continuous). 6. Values specified columns contain special characters. 7. Verify specified reference target values exist corresponding sample metadata column. 8. Issue warning reference target levels identical.

title: Read and validate a contrasts file against sample metadata — read_contrasts
code: read_contrasts(   filename,   samples,   variable_column = "variable",   reference_column = "reference",   target_column = "target",   blocking_column = "blocking",   convert_to_list = FALSE,   validate_design = TRUE )

title: Read and validate a contrasts file against sample metadata — read_contrasts
text: filename Contrasts file samples Data frame sample information variable_column Column contrasts file referencing sample sheet column reference_column Column contrast file referencing reference level sample sheet variable target_column Column contrast file referencing target level sample sheet variable blocking_column Colon-separated column contrast file referencing sample sheet variables used blocking factors convert_to_list Convert output list used internally shinyngs? validate_design Validate design matrix (check NAs, full rank, numeric columns, special characters)? Set FALSE skip checks.

text: output Validated contrasts data frame

title: Server function for the scatterplot module — scatterplot
text: id Module namespace getDatamatrix Reactive supplying matrix. using external controls match one supplied scatterplotcontrols getThreedee reactive defining whether plot 3D. set NULL (default), scatterplotcontrols module called create set inputs supply value axes etc. getXAxis NULL, getThreedee reactive, reactive supplying integer specifying column matrix supplied getDatamatrix used axis. getYAxis NULL, getThreedee reactive, reactive supplying integer specifying column matrix supplied getDatamatrix used axis. getZAxis NULL, getThreedee reactive, reactive supplying integer specifying column matrix supplied getDatamatrix used axis. getShowLabels NULL, getThreedee reactive, reactive supplying logical defining whether labels shown points. getPointSize NULL, getThreedee reactive, reactive supplying integer point size pass plotly. getPalette optional palette colors, one level getColorby. getColorby reactive returning factor definining groups points colored. getTitle reactive expression supplying title. getLabels reactive supplying list labels use instead row names getDatamatrix() allow_3d Passed scatterplotcontrolsInput dermine user allowed create 3D plots. x Passed scatterplotcontrolsInput determine produces input field selecting x axis. value supplied parameter cause hidden field generated instead select, useful scatter plots need user select axes (default: NA). y Passed scatterplotcontrolsInput determine produces input field selecting y axis. value supplied parameter cause hidden field generated instead select, useful scatter plots need user select axes (default: NA). z Passed scatterplotcontrolsInput determine produces input field selecting z axis. value supplied parameter cause hidden field generated instead select, useful scatter plots need user select axes (default: NA). getLines Reactive returning data frame defining lines drawn. Three columns required: name, x y, two rows every value name. two rows represent start end line.

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS. — shinyngs
text: shinyngs defines two new data   structures. first, ExploratorySummarizedExperiment simply   takes SummarizedExperiment, adds slots allow extra description   around experiments. extra slots compulsory, however,   simple SummarizedExperiment can converted directly   ExploratorySummarizedExperiment:  single ExploratorySummarizedExperiment limited one   set features, ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList   devised allow incorporation feature sets. example, might   want visualise expression terms transcripts genes.   ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList just list   ExploratorySummarizedExperiments, additional slots   pertaining study whole. use shinyngs, data assumed formats.   terribly happy structure slots objects,   may open 'improvement' future.

Modules allow essentially object-oriented coding Shiny, hiving   code namespaces can re-used different parts   application without interfering . shinyngs many   modules provide various functionalities.   module functions currently exported, currently   intended use outside shinyngs. may change.

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS. — shinyngs
text: Usage modules defined shinyngs envisaged   predominantly via prepare_app. modules, passes   arguments simpleApp, simply takes   ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList module name, attempts   call UI server functions module, creating simple   sidebar layout. Taking dendro app using airway example   data:  apps, example rnaseq require complex layouts   use simpleApp. prepare_app returns list UI server applications components.   Shiny's shinyApp must called actually run   app: shiny::shinyApp(ui = app$ui, server = app$server).

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS. — shinyngs
text: three varieties module shinyngs. Top-level modules rnaseq provide complex data mining tools pulling together number modules. sub-modules can also called stand-alone make heatmaps dendrograms, example. modules just provide small components make sense isolation. full set time writing:

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS. — shinyngs
text: chipseq Currently near clone rnaseq, optimised ChIP-seq due course.

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS. — shinyngs
text: modules can also called directly prepare_app: assaydatatable Provide access asay matrices input object boxplot Make quartile/ boxplots dendro Make sample-clustering dendrograms. dexseqplot Differential exon usage plot (relevant slot populated) dexseqtable Differential exon usage table (relevant slot populated) differentialtable Differential table showing summarised group values fold changes etc (contrasts slot populated). experimentaltable Display colData() output selected ExploratorySummarizedExperiment foldchangeplot Plot mean values one condition another (contrasts slot populated) gene Show expression plots available data selected features genesetanalysistable Display content gene_set_analyses slot selected ExploratorySummarizedExperiment genesetbarcodeplot Make barcode plot using selected data limma's barcodeplot heatmap Build heatmaps selected matrix data. default expression matrix, can also sample vs sample correlation heatmaps PCA vs covariate association heatmaps. maplot Uses scatterplot module plot mean expression vs fold change pca Plots 2D 3D PCA loading plots based selected matrix data, using scatterplot module readreports Makes bar plots tables read counts data provided read_reports slot ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList rowmetatable Displays data metadata slot ExploratorySummarizedExperiment object (accessed via mcols) volcanoplot Uses scatterplot module plot fold change vs p value (necessary slots populated

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS. — shinyngs
text: modules valid components applications created modules: barplot Used readreports, example. Requires reactive providing input data contrasts Provides data sample group comparisons, using contrasts slot input object (available) geneselect Provides selection filters assay matrix rows. Used mainly selectmatrix module genesetselect Uses Selectize provide autocomplete field select available gene sets (provided gene_sets slot) groupby Provides UI element choose group_vars SummarizedExperment. Useful coloring PCA etc labelselectfield module provides input allows filtering basis row IDs, labels, data metadata slot ExploratorySummarizedExperiment modal uses Shiny's modalDialog() create overlaid text current panel displays link clicked plotdownload Provides download button displayed plots sampleselect Allows subset columns selected selected assay matrix scatterplot generic module producing scatter plots using Plotly's plot_ly method. Used multiple modules, including foldchangeplot volcanoplot scatterplotcontrols set controls scatter plots. Separation controls module allows used multiple scatter plot instances. example, PCA loading plots can run set controls selectmatrix Provides functionality central shinyngs. Uses filtes experiment assay, along sampleselect geneselect, select parts input matrices use shinyngs modules simpletable useful generic module takes reactive returns matrix, displays table produces download button summarizematrix Module takes matrix factor groups columns, summarises supplied method, mean default

title: Interactive downstream analysis with ShinyNGS. — shinyngs

title: Make a boxplot with coloring by experimental variable — static_boxplot
text: plotmatrices Expression/ data matrix, named list thereof experiment Annotation columns plotmatrix colorby Column name experiment specifying boxes colored palette Palette colors, one unique value derived colorby. expressiontype Expression type use y axis label whisker_distance Passed geom_boxplot coef, controlling length whiskers. what: Make a faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — static_topgene_boxplots
title: Make a faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — static_topgene_boxplots
text: Draws one boxplot facet per gene, samples grouped condition, optional beeswarm overlay individual points. function renders: gene ranking, significance filtering q values computed upstream caller (e.g. contrasts module).

title: Make a faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — static_topgene_boxplots
code: static_topgene_boxplots(   assay,   groupby,   genes,   annotations = NULL,   labels = NULL,   beeswarm = TRUE,   ncol = NULL,   palette = NULL,   palette_name = COLORBLIND_PALETTE_NAME,   expressiontype = "expression",   base_size = 11,   should_transform = NULL )

title: Make a faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — static_topgene_boxplots
text: assay Numeric matrix, genes (rows) samples (columns) groupby Vector group labels, one per column assay genes Character vector row names assay facet , order facets appear. Also used look values assay, must match row names even labels supplied. annotations Optional named character vector keyed values genes, rendered per-facet annotation (e.g. q value string) labels Optional named character vector keyed values genes, used facet title place raw gene identifier (e.g. gene symbol genes holds Ensembl IDs). Genes missing labels fall back raw identifier. genes still drives lookup assay matching annotations. beeswarm Overlay individual points using geom_quasirandom? ncol Number facet columns. Defaults min(3, length(genes)) palette Palette colours, one unique value groupby palette_name Valid R color palette name expressiontype Expression type use y axis label base_size Passed ggplot's theme() should_transform boolean indicating log2 transformation applied. TRUE, log2 transformation applied unconditionally. FALSE, transformation applied. NULL, conditional transformation based threshold applied.

title: Make a faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — static_topgene_boxplots
text: output ggplot output

title: Make a faceted boxplot of the top differential genes in a contrast — static_topgene_boxplots
code: require(airway) data(airway, package = "airway") mat <- assays(airway)[[1]][1:4, ] groupby <- as.character(colData(airway)$dex) static_topgene_boxplots(mat, groupby, rownames(mat))

code: sample_metadata_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(sample = paste0("s", 1:4), condition = rep(c("treated", "control"), each = 2)),   sample_metadata_file, row.names = FALSE ) mat <- matrix(1:12, nrow = 3, dimnames = list(paste0("gene", 1:3), paste0("s", 1:4))) matrix_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(gene_id = rownames(mat), mat, check.names = FALSE),   matrix_file, row.names = FALSE ) validate_inputs(   samples_metadata = sample_metadata_file,   assay_files = matrix_file,   sample_id_col = "sample" ) #> Reading sample sheet at /tmp/Rtmp1SnI1R/file29ce4a4f01ca.csv with ID col sample #> Reading assay matrix /tmp/Rtmp1SnI1R/file29ce1ed06950.csv and validating against samples and features (if supplied) #> ...  /tmp/Rtmp1SnI1R/file29ce1ed06950.csv matrix good #> $`/tmp/Rtmp1SnI1R/file29ce4a4f01ca.csv` #>    sample condition #> s1     s1   treated #> s2     s2   treated #> s3     s3   control #> s4     s4   control #>  #> $assays #> $assays$`/tmp/Rtmp1SnI1R/file29ce1ed06950.csv` #>       s1 s2 s3 s4 #> gene1  1  4  7 10 #> gene2  2  5  8 11 #> gene3  3  6  9 12 #>  #>

title: shinyngs (development version)
text: Plotting: plotly_scatterplot() → interactive_scatterplot(), plotly_barchart() → interactive_barchart(), plotly_boxplot() → interactive_boxplot(), plotly_barcodeplot() → interactive_barcodeplot(), plotly_cluster_profiles() → interactive_cluster_profiles(), plotly_clusteringDendrogram() → interactive_clustering_dendrogram(), plotly_count_barplot() → interactive_count_barplot(), plotly_densityplot() → interactive_densityplot(), plotly_illumina_control_probes() → interactive_illumina_control_probes(), plotly_pca_metadata_heatmap() → interactive_pca_metadata_heatmap(), plotly_pca_variance_heatmap() → interactive_pca_variance_heatmap(), plotly_quartiles() → interactive_quartiles(), plotly_screeplot() → interactive_screeplot(), plotly_topgene_boxplots() → interactive_topgene_boxplots(), plotly_upset() → interactive_upset(), interactiveHeatmap() → interactive_heatmap(), ggplot_boxplot() → static_boxplot(), ggplot_densityplot() → static_densityplot(), ggplot_topgene_boxplots() → static_topgene_boxplots(), clusteringDendrogram() → clustering_dendrogram(), calculateDendrogram() → calculate_dendrogram(). static_scatterplot() unchanged. Matrix/stats utilities: colMedians() → col_medians(), colGeomMeans() → col_geom_means(), madScore() → mad_score(), bootstrapMedian() → bootstrap_median(), summarizeMatrix() → summarize_matrix(), summarySE() → summary_se(), singleValidMatrix() → single_valid_matrix(), interleaveColumns() → interleave_columns(), simpleSplit() → simple_split(), calculateDist() → calculate_dist(), foldChange() → fold_change(), runClustering() → run_clustering(), selectVariableGenes() → select_variable_genes(), compilePCAData() → compile_pca_data(). String/general utilities: ucfirst() → capitalize_first(), na.replace() → na_replace(), nlines() → count_lines(), getExtension() → file_extension(), getSeparator() → guess_separator(), splitStringToFixedwidthLines() → split_string_to_fixed_width_lines(), stringsToNamedVector() → strings_to_named_vector(), checkListIsSubset() → check_list_is_subset(), pushToList() → push_to_list(), prettifyGeneSetName() → prettify_gene_set_name(), prettifyVariablename() → prettify_variable_name(), idToLabel() → id_to_label(), convertIds() → convert_ids(), chooseGroupingVariables() → choose_grouping_variables(), makeColorScale() → make_color_scale(), validateOrCatch() → validate_or_catch(). Shiny UI helpers builders: hiddenInput() → hidden_input(), inlineField() → inline_field(), withHelpIcon() → with_help_icon(), prepareApp() → prepare_app(), eselistFromYAML() → eselist_from_yaml(), eselistfromConfig() → eselist_from_config(). S4 class constructors (ExploratorySummarizedExperiment, ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList) unchanged — PascalCase stays convention , matching Bioconductor practice.

title: shinyngs (development version)
text: pkgdown site gains screenshots diagrams: home-page gallery, screenshot almost every analysis panel module/panel catalogue (including platform-specific DEXSeq Illumina array QC panels), architecture diagrams showing input data becomes app (data model article) modules compose one (developer guide). single monolithic vignette split set task-focused articles: getting started, data model, building app files, command-line interface reference, module/panel catalogue, reusing components inside outside Shiny, theming shareable views, developer guide. pkgdown site gains articles menu changelog. Added runnable examples standalone plotting API (interactive_scatterplot(), static_scatterplot(), interactive_heatmap(), interactive_densityplot(), interactive_barcodeplot()), object constructors (ExploratorySummarizedExperiment(), ExploratorySummarizedExperimentList()), file readers (read_matrix() friends) several compute helpers.

title: shinyngs (development version)
text: interactive_scatterplot() gains opt-colorby_menu dropdown switching colouring variable within single self-contained widget, plus xrange/yrange arguments pinning axis ranges (e.g. symmetric volcano axis). New standalone, report-callable plotting functions extracted Shiny modules: interactive_pca_metadata_heatmap(), interactive_upset(), interactive_cluster_profiles(), interactive_barchart(), interactive_illumina_control_probes(), interactive_count_barplot(), interactive_screeplot() interactive_topgene_boxplots() / static_topgene_boxplots(). scree plot available PCA panel. generic category-counts plot feature sample metadata.

title: shinyngs (development version)
text: Every interactive plot’s download button honours app-wide PNG/SVG format toggle, plot can exported vector SVG publication. Dropped reformulas data.table dependencies. Progress warning logging object construction validation now goes message()/warning() rather print(), can suppressed captured R’s condition system.

title: shinyngs (development version)
text: Removed unused exported function geom_mean(); geometric means computed directly col_geom_means(). Declared grDevices Imports (already used). Normalised handful module house-style outliers: shared differential-scatter helper trio now differentialscatterInput() / differentialscatterOutput() / differentialscatterLogic() (lowercase, misleading Server suffix non-module logic helper); contrasts.R now uses validate(need()) throughout instead mix req(); plotlyOutput() heights consistently use quoted "NNNpx" string fixed heights bare numeric computed ones.
